title: narcissus hispanicus subsp. obvallaris
intro: Narcissus hispanicus subsp. obvallaris, commonly known as the Pyrenean narcissus or Pyrenean daffodil, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the Amaryllidaceae family. It is traditionally used in herbal medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which have been found to be effective in treating various health issues, including respiratory problems and skin conditions. The plant contains alkaloids, including galantamine, which has been shown to have potential health benefits, including improved cognitive function and cardiovascular health. The herbal preparations of Narcissus hispanicus subsp. obvallaris include infusions, tinctures, and ointments, which can be used to treat a range of health issues, although its use should be guided by a healthcare professional.
